rulings.Case Title: ROSE CLIVER VS. ESTATE OF BERTHA C.WYRSCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) PES-10-293367 WITH CASE CGC-08-480854
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 11, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFFS ROSE CLIVER, and, MURRY RICHARDSON MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) PES-10-293367 WITH CASE CGC-08-480854. Is granted, Case #293367 designated lead case number. No opposition filed. Matter to remain in Probate Department. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JUDY WILLIAMS VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 11, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIAN'S SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD AMENDED DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. is continued to 6/18/2010 per agreement of the parties. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TEXT 100, INC. VS. IREX HOLDINGS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Settlement Agreemen; Declaration Of Robert L. Shepard In Support Of Motion; P/A In Support Of Motion; Proposes Judgment; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 11, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF TEXT 100, INC. Motion to Compel Settlement Agreement. Is denied. Court lacks subject matter jurisdiction. See Hagan Engineering vs. Mills (2003) 115 Cal.App. 4th 1004. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY KHACHATURIAN VS. MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 11, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO. INC., D. MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, and, ROB FORD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to all causes of action regarding Curhan and Ford for plaintiff to specifically plead allegations against them. As to the other defendants, sustained with leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 5th causes of action. Fraud must be plead with specificity and resulting damages must be adequately plead. As to 3rd cause of action, allege specific provisions of the Corporations Code that have been violated. Overruled as to 6th cause of action (unjust enrichment.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY KHACHATURIAN VS. MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 11, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO., INC., D. MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, and, ROB FORD MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, as moot in view of court's ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SEIKI OKADA VS. FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 11, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC., and, KONDAUR CAPITAL CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must comply with California Rules of Court 2.112. Plaintiff must allege facts to support legally recognized causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY STORAGE VS. UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner City Storage'S Motion For Judgment On Peremptory Writ Of Mandate (Continued From 06/10/10 To 06/11/10)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 11, 2010, line 9, Petitioner City Storage's Motion for Judgment on Peremptory Writ of Mandate (Continued From 06/10/10 To 06/11/10). Is denied. The record does not show that Petitioner sufficiently warned Gibson that his tardiness would result in termination. =(302/CWW)
